job_to_be_done: Track corporate filings, macro developments, and EOD flows without sensational news spin or noise.
current_tools: Moneycontrol Pro, Economic Times Prime, screener.in, Trendlyne
pain_vs_incumbents: Intrusive ad bloat, keyword-matched news tagging errors, and delayed reporting of market-moving announcements.
trust_bar: High (official exchange filings and audited statements)

job_to_be_done: Relocate capital from options to cash equities, tracking high-precision corporate announcements and sector-level flows to identify short-term momentum.
current_tools: Kite, Groww, TradingView, Telegram news channels
pain_vs_incumbents: Lack of real-time broker outage data, delayed global market news, high-frequency noise, and high STT costs in options.
willingness_to_pay: Medium-Low (prefers free; will pay ₹500 - ₹1,000/year for filing/flow alerts)
trust_bar: Medium (relies on institutional flow ratios and swift alerts)

job_to_be_done: Backtest and run systematic trading algorithms using structured, historical company-level news sentiment, corporate actions, and flow events.
current_tools: Heckyl APIs, FactSet, direct exchange tick APIs, custom web scrapers
pain_vs_incumbents: Steep pricing cliff (institutional feeds cost ₹1.2L–₹3L/year), poor NER precision (mistagging 'IT'), and lack of API access in mid-tier tools.
willingness_to_pay: High (₹5,000 - ₹10,000/month API subscription; up to ₹5L for dataset licensing)
trust_bar: Extreme (requires near-100% NER precision and zero-false-positives database)

job_to_be_done: Review long-term portfolio performance and keep up-to-date with general macro trends and regulatory shifts affecting personal finance.
current_tools: Groww, Zerodha Coin, Finshots, YouTube creators
pain_vs_incumbents: Information overload, sensationalist financial journalism, and complex regulatory jargon.
willingness_to_pay: Low (prefers free digests and educational explainers)
trust_bar: High (prefers regulated brokers and highly followed creators)

job_to_be_done: Scalp derivatives using options chains, Greeks, open interest, and technical charts on tight latency constraints.
current_tools: Sensibull, Opstra, Kite, Angel One, TradingView
pain_vs_incumbents: High STT costs (effective April 1, 2026), exchange expiry restrictions, broker system outages, and market-maker execution speed.
willingness_to_pay: Low (requires brokerage integrations and real-time execution speeds)
trust_bar: Low (often relies on informal Telegram/YouTube influencers)

job_to_be_done: Monitor macro liquidity, execute large block deals, and write institutional research reports under sub-millisecond execution constraints.
current_tools: Bloomberg Terminal, Cogencis, Reuters Eikon, institutional desks
pain_vs_incumbents: High licensing costs (₹1.5L to ₹25L/year) but willing to pay for complete, real-time data coverage.

job_to_be_done: Track public market sentiment, policy revisions, corporate governance crises, and economic data prints for reporting.
current_tools: PIB press releases, SEBI portal, Twitter, Telegram, Economic Times
pain_vs_incumbents: Difficulty in identifying underlying market narrative shifts and separating genuine public sentiment from paid/manipulated PR.
